items.title: 5 Shot Types Every Filmmaker Needs to Know
items.caption:
Master these 5 essential shot types and transform your storytelling 🎬

1. Wide Shot — establishes location
2. Medium Shot — shows body language
3. Close-Up — captures emotion
4. Extreme Close-Up — intense detail
5. Over-the-Shoulder — dialogue anchor

Save this for your next shoot! 📌

#filmmaking #cinematography #filmschool #indiefilm #framecoach

items.title: Rule of Thirds Explained
items.caption:
The Rule of Thirds changed how I frame every shot 📐

Divide your frame into 9 equal sections. Place your subject at an intersection point — not the center.

This creates natural tension and visual interest.

#filmtok #filmmaking101 #cinematography #framecoach #filmschool

items.title: Lighting Ratios: 1:1, 2:1, 4:1, 8:1
items.caption:
Lighting ratio determines mood. Here's the cheat sheet 💡

1:1 — Flat, no shadow (interviews, beauty)
2:1 — Subtle shadow (natural look)
4:1 — Defined shadow (drama)
8:1 — Deep shadow (noir, tension)

Which ratio is your go-to? Drop it below 👇

#filmmaking #lighting #cinematography #indiefilm #framecoach

items.title: Why 180-Degree Rule Matters in Every Scene
items.caption:
Cross this line and confuse your audience 🎬

The 180-degree rule keeps screen direction consistent in dialogue scenes. Both cameras stay on the same side of an imaginary line between subjects.

Break it intentionally to create disorientation.

#filmtok #filmmaking #directing #cinematography #framecoach

items.title: Color Grading: Teal and Orange Explained
items.caption:
Why does every blockbuster look the same? Teal & Orange 🎨

Skin tones are orange. Shadows shift teal. The complementary contrast creates a cinematic look that pops.

Here's how to do it in any color grader:
→ Pull shadows to teal
→ Push highlights to warm
→ Skin tone: use Hue vs Saturation curves

#colorgrading #filmmaking #cinematography #davinciresolve #framecoach

items.title: Frame Rate Guide: 24fps vs 30fps vs 60fps
items.caption:
Pick the wrong frame rate and your video looks off 📹

24fps — Cinematic look (films, narrative)
30fps — Standard broadcast (YouTube, TV)
60fps — Smooth motion (sports, gaming)
120fps — Slow motion playback at 30/60fps

Most creators default to 30fps. Narrative filmmakers use 24.

#filmmaking #videography #cinematography #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: The Best Budget Lenses for Indie Filmmakers
items.caption:
Cinema glass on an indie budget 🎥

Top budget lenses under $300:
→ 7Artisans 35mm f/1.4
→ Rokinon Cine DS 24mm
→ Meike 85mm f/1.8
→ Viltrox 56mm f/1.4

All manual focus. All cinematic. All affordable.

#filmtok #filmmaking #gear #lenses #framecoach

items.title: Sound Design Basics Every Filmmaker Must Know
items.caption:
90% of your audience hears before they see 🎙

5 sound design basics:
1. Record clean dialogue first — fix it in post is a myth
2. Room tone: always record 30s of silence on set
3. Foley adds realism (footsteps, clothes)
4. Ambience fills the world (wind, city, nature)
5. Music sets emotion — use it sparingly

#filmmaking #sounddesign #filmschool #indiefilm #framecoach

items.title: Handheld vs Tripod vs Gimbal — When to Use Each
items.caption:
Camera movement tells a story. Pick the wrong one and it breaks immersion.

Handheld — intimacy, urgency, documentary feel
Tripod — stability, formal compositions, authority
Gimbal — smooth motion, following action, commercial
Dolly/Slider — controlled drama, cinematic reveals

Which one do you default to?

#filmtok #filmmaking #cameramovement #cinematography #framecoach

items.title: 3-Point Lighting Setup Step by Step
items.caption:
The 3-point lighting setup every filmmaker should master 💡

Step 1: Key light — main light source, 45° from subject
Step 2: Fill light — softer, opposite side, reduces shadows
Step 3: Back light — separates subject from background

Optional: Background light for depth

Save this as your lighting template!

#filmmaking #lighting #filmschool #cinematography #framecoach

items.title: Aspect Ratios and What They Communicate
items.caption:
Your aspect ratio is a creative decision, not a default setting 📐

16:9 — Standard widescreen, modern/neutral
2.39:1 — Anamorphic scope, epic cinema
4:3 — Vintage, nostalgic, indie aesthetic
1:1 — Social media, Instagram native
9:16 — Mobile-first, TikTok/Reels

Match ratio to story intent.

#filmmaking #cinematography #composition #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: How to Write a One-Page Film Treatment
items.caption:
Get your film idea on paper in one page — treatment format 📝

Structure:
→ Logline (1-2 sentences)
→ Tone and visual style
→ Main character + want vs need
→ Act 1: Setup and inciting incident
→ Act 2: Conflict escalation
→ Act 3: Climax and resolution

Investors and producers read treatments, not scripts.

#filmmaking #screenwriting #preproduction #indiefilm #framecoach

items.title: DaVinci Resolve Keyboard Shortcuts Cheat Sheet
items.caption:
Speed up your edit with these DaVinci Resolve shortcuts ⚡

J/K/L — Shuttle back/stop/forward
I/O — Set In/Out points
D — Delete clip (ripple)
A — Selection tool
B — Blade tool
Ctrl+Z — Undo
Ctrl+D — Duration
Ctrl+Shift+C — Color page

Save this! You'll use it every session.

#davinciresolve #videoediting #filmmaking #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: Depth of Field: How to Get Cinematic Blur
items.caption:
Bokeh is not magic — it's physics 📸

3 ways to get shallow depth of field:
1. Wider aperture (lower f-number: f/1.8, f/2.8)
2. Longer focal length (85mm, 135mm)
3. Get closer to subject + push background farther

Your kit lens at f/3.5 will not give you that cinematic blur. Get a 50mm f/1.8.

#filmtok #filmmaking #cinematography #camera #framecoach

items.title: Shot List Template for Your Next Short Film
items.caption:
Never wing a shoot again. Use a shot list 📋

Every shot needs:
→ Scene number
→ Shot number
→ Shot type (WS, MS, CU)
→ Camera angle
→ Movement (static, pan, dolly)
→ Lens (focal length)
→ Notes (emotion, key action)

I'll drop a free template in the comments.

#filmmaking #preproduction #shortfilm #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: Cinematic Color Palettes: Wes Anderson Style
items.caption:
Wes Anderson's palette is instantly recognizable 🎨

Key elements:
→ Pastel primaries: pink, yellow, mint
→ Symmetrical framing enforces color balance
→ Warm midtones with cool shadows
→ Muted saturation — nothing screams
→ Period props to tie palette together

Create a look, not just a grade.

#filmmaking #colorgrading #wesanderson #cinematography #framecoach

items.title: Interview Setup: How to Frame a Talking Head
items.caption:
Your documentary interviews look amateur — here's the fix 🎙

Setup checklist:
☐ Subject looking slightly off-camera (not at lens)
☐ Eyes at upper third of frame
☐ Background out of focus (open aperture)
☐ Practical light visible in background for depth
☐ Lavalier mic hidden in clothing
☐ 2nd camera for cutaways

#filmmaking #documentary #directing #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: What is Exposure Triangle and Why It Matters
items.caption:
Master the exposure triangle and never blow out a shot again 📐

3 settings that control exposure:
→ ISO: sensor sensitivity (higher = brighter but noisier)
→ Aperture: lens opening (lower f = more light + blur)
→ Shutter Speed: how long sensor captures light

For video: shutter = 2x frame rate (180° rule)
24fps → 1/50s shutter

#filmtok #filmmaking #cinematography #camera #framecoach

items.title: B-Roll Checklist: What to Capture on Every Shoot
items.caption:
No B-roll = no edit. Here's what to always capture 🎬

Environment:
☐ Establishing wide shots
☐ Location details (signs, textures, objects)

Action:
☐ Hands doing things
☐ Feet walking
☐ Eyes reacting

Cutaways:
☐ Reaction shots
☐ Insert shots (objects referenced in dialogue)

Shoot 3x more B-roll than you think you need.

#filmmaking #directing #broll #filmschool #framecoach

items.title: Screenplay Format: The Industry Standard
items.caption:
Format your screenplay wrong and it screams amateur 📝

Industry standard (Final Draft / Fountain):
→ 12pt Courier font
→ 1-inch margins (left: 1.5")
→ Scene headings: INT./EXT. LOCATION - DAY/NIGHT
→ Action lines: present tense, only what we SEE
→ Character names: CENTERED, ALL CAPS before dialogue
→ 1 page ≈ 1 minute of screen time

Use Fade In or Highland 2 (free).

#filmmaking #screenwriting #screenplay #indiefilm #framecoach
